tema: Will vs Going to — Plans, Decisions & Promises

banco_oraciones.oracion: I'm going to travel to Cartagena in December — I bought the tickets.
You know what? I'll go with you! Decided. Just now.
I think Colombia will win tomorrow.
Look at those clouds — it's going to rain.
I won't tell anyone. I promise.
We aren't going to cook tonight; the fridge is empty.
It won't be easy, but we'll try.
Will you help me with this exercise?
What are you going to do in December?
Do you think it will rain tomorrow?
Will you come to the party? Yes, I will.
Are you going to study tonight? No, I'm not.
Will it rain? I think it will.

interaccion: Sofía: In December **I'm going to travel** to Cartagena. Tickets: bought. Hotel: booked. Spreadsheet: ready.
Ben: Sounds fun! You know what? **I'll go** with you! Decided. Just now.
Kenji: And that is exactly the difference. Sofía planned it — Ben just decided it this second.
Abuela Carmen: And I **will always be** here waiting... with soup. That's a promise.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: Look at those clouds — it will rain.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Use 'going to' for plans you already made and predictions you can see coming; use 'will' for decisions made right now, opinions about the future, and promises.

tabla.titulo: will vs going to — who decides, and when?
filas: I'm going to travel in December (tickets bought)
You know what? I'll go with you!
Look at those clouds — it's going to rain
I'll help you. I won't tell anyone.
We aren't going to cook tonight

ejercicios.pregunta: The phone is ringing! — Don't worry, I ___ answer it.
Look at those clouds. It ___ rain.
I bought the tickets. We ___ travel in December.
I think our team ___ win tomorrow.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'Will' is used for decisions made at the moment of speaking.
'Going to' is only for predictions without evidence.

ejercicios.texto: She ___ (be) going to study medicine — she has already chosen the university.
You know what? I ___ (help) you right now.

ejercicios.respuesta: I think it will rain tomorrow
Are you going to tell her

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Look at those clouds — it will rain.
ejercicios.respuesta: Look at those clouds — it's going to rain.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: We will to travel in December.

ejercicios.oracion_base: They are going to move to Cali.
ejercicios.respuesta: Are they going to move to Cali?

ejercicios.oracion_base: Will you come to my party?

ejercicios.pregunta: Will you practice English tomorrow? (short answer)
Are you going to sleep early tonight? (short answer, No)

guion: Sofía: OK, December trip. I'm going to book the boat tour tomorrow.
Ben: I'll carry all the bags! I'm strong and reliable.
Kenji: I think you'll drop the bags in the sea, Ben.
Abuela Carmen: Children, I'll make the sancocho for the trip. And Ben — you aren't going to carry the pot.

comprension.pregunta: What is Sofía going to book tomorrow?

reading.texto: Ben's New Year plan, written in his notebook: 'This year is going to be different. I'm going to learn salsa — I have already paid for ten classes. I'm going to save money for a new camera. And I'm not going to lose my phone again.' Under the plan, there is a phone number and a note in different ink: 'Kenji, if you find this notebook, I'll buy you dinner. I lost it on the bus. Again. Some things will never change.'

preguntas.afirmacion: Ben is going to learn salsa.
He has already paid for the classes.
Ben is going to buy a new phone.
Kenji wrote the New Year plan.
